key_themes: Strong Q1 2026 earnings beat with revenue +8.5% and EPS $1.61 vs $1.55 expected
Turnaround in North American snacks (Frito-Lay/Doritos/Lay's) via price cuts driving 2% volume growth and 300M new consumption occasions
Reaffirmed full-year 2026 guidance (2-4% organic revenue, 4-6% core EPS growth)
Shareholder returns with 4% dividend increase (54th consecutive) and $1B buybacks
Macro/geopolitical risks (inflation, Iran conflict) but strong supply chain resilience and innovation pipeline
summary: PepsiCo delivered a clear earnings beat in Q1 2026, with net revenue of $19.44B (organic +2.6%) and adjusted EPS of $1.61, as strategic price reductions on core snacks brands successfully reversed prior volume weakness in North America and drove meaningful demand recovery. Management reaffirmed its full-year outlook, highlighted innovation in protein/fiber/hydration products, and noted no major supply disruptions from geopolitical tensions, though it flagged expected inflation. Shares rose post-earnings, reflecting improved investor confidence in the pricing reset and defensive qualities of the business.[[1]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/pepsico-pep-q1-2026-earnings.html)[[1]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/pepsico-pep-q1-2026-earnings.html)
sources: 15+ recent news articles and earnings reports (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, company release, Seeking Alpha), 20+ X posts on earnings reaction, volume recovery, and analyst commentary (mostly from April 16-18 2026)
